title: salix × ambigua nothosubsp. ambigua
intro: Salix × ambigua nothosubsp. ambigua, commonly known as the hybrid willow or white willow, is a plant species that is a hybrid of Salix alba and Salix fragilis. The bark of this plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects due to the presence of salicin, a compound similar to aspirin. The health benefits of Salix × ambigua nothosubsp. ambigua include reducing fever and relieving pain, as well as aiding in the treatment of rheumatism and other inflammatory conditions. The plant is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas and infusions, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits, although its use should be under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
